Annotation

Conclusion and Recommendations based on this report and previous findings known to us
The results indicate significant renal dysfunction (markedly elevated creatinine, proteinuria, microalbuminuria), metabolic acidosis (low CO₂), and hypoglycemia with low HbA1c. Urinalysis shows nephrotic-range proteinuria, ketonuria, glucosuria, and abnormal urine appearance, suggesting possible diabetic nephropathy or another glomerular pathology. Elevated LDL cholesterol increases cardiovascular risk. Immediate nephrology evaluation is recommended.
